name: Oatcakes (Bakestone Recipes)
recipeInstructions:
1. Grind regular rolled oats slightly in a blender or grinder.
2. Mix the oatmeal, salt, and bicarbonate of soda in a bowl.
3. Make a well in the center. Pour in the melted fat and add enough hot water to make a stiff dough that can be squeezed into a ball.
4. Sprinkle the board and your hands with oatmeal and knead the mixture until there are no cracks.
5. Flatten the ball and roll it out into a round just under 1/4 inch thick.
6. Invert a plate on top and trim off the ragged edges, which can be added to the next batch of dough to be rolled.
7. Cut the round into quarters.
8. Lift them onto a warmed and greased bakestone and cook over moderate heat for 20 minutes or until the triangles curl at the corners.
9. Turn and cook the other side for 5 minutes, or finish under a moderate grill.
10. Store the oatcakes in an airtight tin and toast under a moderate grill or in the oven before serving.
These are served with honey, marmalade, or jam for breakfast, or with crowdie (a slightly sweet Highland Scots cottage cheese) for tea. They are especially good with fried herring or smoked haddock. Not to mention any kind of caviar you can get your hands on.
